result.chapter_summary: While praising the intent of the Common Core State Standards, Hirsch argues that they have failed to improve student outcomes or close gaps because they lack specific, sequenced content. He proposes that for any curriculum to be effective and equitable, it must adhere to three criteria: coherence, commonality, and specificity, as demonstrated by top-performing nations like Singapore.

claims.text: The reformers, philanthropists, and governors who created the Common Core State Standards are public benefactors.
claims.source_passage: The thought leaders, philanthropists, and governors who have created and supported the initiative are public benefactors to whom, if the standards get some concrete content, we will owe high gratitude.

claims.text: The Common Core standards will only mark a permanent improvement in American schools if they incorporate concrete content.
claims.source_passage: And once these multistate standards put flesh on their bones and manage to achieve their proper form, they will mark a permanent change for the better in our schools and country.

claims.text: The Common Core standards have not yet raised student scores in language, math, and science, nor narrowed achievement gaps.
claims.source_passage: But the Common Core standards have not yet raised our students’ scores in language, math, and science, nor have they narrowed the gaps between advantaged and disadvantaged students.

claims.text: The primary reason the Common Core standards have not improved outcomes is that they lack specific content.
claims.source_passage: But the main reason the standards have not had much effect on outcomes is that they are not yet specific. They lack content, and only specific content can induce the shared knowledge needed to sustain a nation.

claims.text: Only specific content can produce the shared knowledge required to sustain a nation.
claims.source_passage: They lack content, and only specific content can induce the shared knowledge needed to sustain a nation.

claims.text: Mathematical learning is strictly sequential; for example, students cannot reliably learn multiplication without prior knowledge of addition.
claims.source_passage: They can’t reliably learn about multiplication until they have already learned about addition.

claims.text: A lack of shared prior knowledge in a classroom leads to instructional inefficiency, student frustration, and behavioral problems.
claims.source_passage: More likely, they would sit in the class, fall further behind, become frustrated, and create some behavioral problems. Or the teacher could slow down the whole class while addition is reexplained, a practice that is boring to the better-informed students.

claims.text: A classroom can only function as an effective speech community if all members possess the relevant background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: We can create specific standards, so each classroom becomes a speech community whose members all understand what is being said, because they all possess the needed relevant background knowledge.

claims.text: Every academic subject, including art history, math, and engineering, is fundamentally language-dependent.
claims.source_passage: Every subject of schooling is language dependent—even art history, math, and engineering.

claims.text: Human thinking is coded symbolically in the neocortex and these symbols can be translated into words.
claims.source_passage: Much of our thinking is coded symbolically in the neocortex, and those symbols can be translated into words.

claims.text: Educational topics cannot be randomly chosen but must be carefully sequenced and seeded beforehand.
claims.source_passage: Topics cannot be randomly chosen but must be seeded beforehand. Such careful sequencing cannot readily be accomplished by a diversity of topics in the same classroom.

claims.text: A successful elementary curriculum must meet three criteria: coherence, commonality, and specificity.
claims.source_passage: A good elementary curriculum in any school, district, or nation must therefore exhibit three criteria to have a truly significant effect on the quality and equity of schooling. Coherence over time is essential... commonality is essential... and specificity is necessary to ensure commonality.

claims.text: Curricular coherence over time is necessary for cumulative learning.
claims.source_passage: Coherence over time is essential for ensuring that all students have the needed background knowledge for cumulative learning.

claims.text: Commonality of curriculum is essential to ensure student inclusion and form a classroom speech community.
claims.source_passage: commonality is essential to ensure inclusion of everyone and the forming of the classroom into a speech community

claims.text: Current Common Core State Standards lack the necessary coherence, commonality, and specificity required for excellent and equitable results.
claims.source_passage: But these qualities are not yet elements of the Common Core State Standards. This means that despite their admirable goals... improvements are needed, especially in the key early grades K–6.

claims.text: Singapore's elementary curriculum strikes a successful balance between content mastery, skills, and values.
claims.source_passage: Singapore’s introductory document for the elementary grades turns out to be a marvelous description of an ideal elementary education, one that strikes a balance of content mastery, skills, and values.

claims.text: Language proficiency is a social art inherently connected to society rather than a set of abstract skills.
claims.source_passage: Rightly understood, language proficiency is a social art inherently connected to society. It holds a given society together, and it is intimately connected to what Durkheim called moralité.

claims.text: The Singaporean approach to science education uses thematic linking across different branches of science to foster a more complete understanding of the world.
claims.source_passage: Rather than chopping up the different branches of science into different grade levels and books, a thematic approach is taken, where concepts from different areas of science, including life science, physical science, chemistry, and physical science are all linked together.

claims.text: Singapore's social studies curriculum is intentionally designed for acculturation and patriotic values rather than just factual transmission.
claims.source_passage: This is hardly a just-the-facts approach. It’s deliberately acculturative and patriotic.

claims.text: Educational coherence and specificity in Singapore are guaranteed by mandating a single, specific, and cumulative textbook series for all schools.
claims.source_passage: The coherence and specificity of the Singaporean elementary schools are guaranteed by a simple administrative device: “You must teach this single textbook and its coherent and specific content.”

claims.text: American educational publishers prioritize profit and speed over the technical accuracy or pedagogical perfection of textbooks.
claims.source_passage: What we’re left with are quickly produced books filled with errors and omissions, and rather than spending the money to perfect a small number of books, publishers have found it more profitable to keep churning out new ones each year.

claims.text: Technical excellence and fairness in education are dependent upon the high degree of coherence and specificity found in curricula like Singapore's.
claims.source_passage: how could we ever achieve the high degree of coherence and specificity necessary for technical excellence and fairness—two outstanding qualities of Singaporean education?

claims.text: American government leaders are unwilling to impose an explicit national plan for education.
claims.source_passage: With the financial motivations of the educational companies and the unwillingness of government leaders to do anything to impose an explicit plan for education

claims.text: The terminology 'Character and Citizenship Education' used in Singapore is more accurate than the American term 'Language Arts' because it recognizes the acculturative nature of language.
claims.source_passage: The terminology is very clear about the formative, acculturative character of shared language and shared knowledge—a much more sophisticated and accurate approach than our “language arts,” whose title implies that language proficiency is a set of abstract skills.

claims.text: Educational publishers prioritize profit over accuracy, resulting in quickly produced books filled with errors and omissions.
claims.source_passage: What we’re left with are quickly produced books filled with errors and omissions, and rather than spending the money to perfect a small number of books, publishers have found it more profitable to keep churning out new ones each year.

claims.text: The financial motivations of educational companies and government inaction prevent the implementation of explicit, coherent educational plans.
claims.source_passage: With the financial motivations of the educational companies and the unwillingness of government leaders to do anything to impose an explicit plan for education, how could we ever achieve the high degree of coherence and specificity necessary for technical excellence and fairness

claims.text: Each US state or a coalition of states must create a specific grade-by-grade topic sequence to ensure good and fair elementary schooling.
claims.source_passage: What we need is for each US state or a coalition of states to create a specific grade-by-grade topic sequence. That is essential for good and fair elementary schooling.

claims.text: Any educational approach that lacks a specific grade-by-grade topic sequence is fundamentally inferior in principle.
claims.source_passage: Any other approach (no matter how politically and commercially attractive) is inferior in principle.

claims.text: American educators follow inferior principles because they inherited and earnestly adhere to incorrect progressive traditions from the early twentieth century.
claims.source_passage: To be fair, our educators have been following inferior principles partly because the progressive traditions that we have inherited from the first half of the twentieth century, though incorrect, are still earnestly adhered to.

claims.text: The unfulfilled goal of the Common Core State Standards is to make education grade-and-topic specific.
claims.source_passage: To make their standards grade-and-topic specific is the still-to-be-accomplished task of the Common Core State Standards.

claims.text: Singapore's top international rankings in reading, math, and science are due to students possessing more knowledge than students in other nations.
claims.source_passage: The technical reason that Singapore now scores at the top in reading, math, and science is that Singaporean students know more than students in other countries.

claims.text: In Singapore, academic performance in reading, math, and science is high and equitable across all socioeconomic levels because knowledge is effectively imparted to all students.
claims.source_passage: A purely random group of its fifteen-year-olds will perform splendidly in reading, math, and science—no matter the educational and economic level of their parents and caregivers. The average Singaporean students know a lot because their schools have imparted knowledge very effectively to all.

claims.text: A content-rich curriculum makes traditional educational standards unnecessary.
claims.source_passage: But as we saw in the case of Singapore, if you have a content-rich curriculum, you don’t need “standards.”

claims.text: Textual complexity is not a scientifically valid criterion for evaluating student reading ability.
claims.source_passage: Textual complexity is not a scientifically valid criterion, because textual complexity may be quite easy to the student when the subject matter is familiar, and textual simplicity quite difficult when the subject matter is unfamiliar.

claims.text: The terms 'complexity' and 'genre' in educational standards incorrectly imply that reading skills are content-independent general skills.
claims.source_passage: The terms “complexity” and “genre” imply the same fallacy about skills—the skill of reading complex texts; the skill of reading novels; the skill of reading lyric poetry. But we now know that these don’t exist as content-independent general skills.

claims.text: Students master required skills naturally through the process of engaging with a coherent, content-rich curriculum.
claims.source_passage: If a “content rich” common curriculum is put into effect intelligently, students will master needed skills in the course of mastering the demands of a challenging, coherent curriculum.

claims.text: The decline in American SAT verbal scores between 1952 and 2012 is caused by a decrease in shared knowledge required for social communication.
claims.source_passage: The superior verbal skills demonstrated by American students on the SAT verbal test back in 1952 owed to their having more of the shared knowledge needed for social communication than the students of 2012. That, not our lack of nonexistent abstract skills, explains the decline of our scores.

claims.text: School administrators currently adhere to constructivist principles and the tradition of romantic individualism.
claims.source_passage: It lacks only the adherence of those who administer our schools, and who have been trained under the late-twentieth-century tradition of romantic individualism. Those who administer our schools under constructivist principles...

claims.text: A coalition of nonpolitical organizations should create explicit topic sequences and free online textbooks to prove the success of a content-rich approach.
claims.source_passage: A group of respectable nonpolitical organizations would need to come together to create a set of very explicit topic sequences in all the subjects to be taught in elementary school. They would take advantage of the internet and put some model grade-by-grade textbooks online

claims.text: State governments should provide model grade-by-grade textbooks and instructional guides online for free to facilitate educational reform.
claims.source_passage: They would take advantage of the internet and put some model grade-by-grade textbooks online, along with useful instructional guides for teachers that could also be printed out for free.

claims.text: Schools that adopt an explicit, integrated approach to knowledge-based curriculum will flourish and see highly enthusiastic students.
claims.source_passage: The schools that used such an explicit, integrated approach would flourish. The children would be highly enthusiastic.

claims.text: An explicit knowledge-based curriculum causes student reading scores to rise significantly.
claims.source_passage: Their reading scores would shoot up.

claims.text: A knowledge-based elementary education enables students from disadvantaged backgrounds to pass selective high school admission exams and become knowledgeable citizens.
claims.source_passage: The eighth-grade graduates would be admitted to selective high schools, for which they would have to pass admission exams. They would become knowledgeable citizens, regardless of their original backgrounds.

claims.text: The return of the common school tradition depends on a state leader successfully demonstrating superior equity and achievement through a content-rich initiative.
claims.source_passage: When her state started surpassing all the others in equity and level of achievement, the common school might finally make a comeback.

claims.text: The non-specialist knowledge base of literate Americans can be effectively translated into a K-6 content sequence.
claims.source_passage: It made sense that this nonspecialist knowledge base of educated Americans could serve as a start. Then it could be broken down into topics that might belong exclusively to high school and college.

claims.text: Curriculum design is a zero-sum process where adding a new topic requires the removal of an equally time-consuming topic.
claims.source_passage: If you put a topic in, you had to remove an equally time-consuming topic.

claims.text: Parents and teachers are dissatisfied with empty, skill-centered preparation for standardized tests.
claims.source_passage: the parents and teachers in his district were hungry for this kind of coherent approach to substance; that they were tired of empty, skill-centered preparation for tests.

claims.text: A content-driven approach to schooling is the primary factor that makes a difference for student success.
claims.source_passage: this approach would ultimately make the key difference for all students.

claims.text: The premature death of Paul Bell represented a significant lost opportunity for American public school reform.
claims.source_passage: And I still think of that unique pioneering effort by someone in high authority as a lost chance for our public schooling, and as the most tragic disappointment of my long journey in educational reform.

claims.text: Language proficiency is a knowledge-drenched skill rather than a natural, inborn skill.
claims.source_passage: abandon the now-known-to-be-incorrect premise that language proficiency is a natural, inborn skill. It is a knowledge-drenched skill.

claims.text: Each utterance requires specific, unstated background information to be understood.
claims.source_passage: Each utterance, to be understood, requires specific, unstated background information.

claims.text: Helter-skelter content based on the child's interests leads to low verbal scores and low levels of educational equity.
claims.source_passage: The evidence shows that helter-skelter content based on the child’s interests leads to low verbal scores and low levels of educational equity

claims.text: Shared knowledge in elementary school enables individuals to escape dominant prejudices and achieve independent thought.
claims.source_passage: all that 'useless' shared knowledge did turn us into quite good readers. And in my case... it enabled me to read An American Dilemma by Gunnar Myrdal, which opened my eyes and allowed me to escape the dominant prejudices around me. That is, it enabled just the opposite of thought control!

claims.text: The civil rights movement was accomplished by people who were intellectually and socially competent due to a fact-based common education.
claims.source_passage: How else can such a massive change for the better as the civil rights movement be accomplished except by people who are intellectually and socially competent?

claims.text: Declining verbal scores in the United States have made the population more susceptible to demagoguery and political polarization.
claims.source_passage: But as the United States’ verbal scores have declined, it has become ever easier for a demagogue to lead us sheep-like against other sheep-like followers.

claims.text: A knowledge-rich national curriculum would produce a powerful and vital political center.
claims.source_passage: Am I saying that a knowledge-rich national curriculum would produce a powerful, vital political center? Yes.

claims.text: State-led curriculum reform should offer a topic sequence for the whole of the elementary curriculum, not just language arts.
claims.source_passage: The resulting curriculum should offer a topic sequence for the whole of the elementary curriculum, not just language arts or some other subject.

claims.text: There is a lack of evidence for the negative predictions that common content leads to thought control or loss of freedom.
claims.source_passage: There is a distinct lack of evidence for these negative predictions. Did any of these things happen earlier when our schools deliberately taught common content? Quite the contrary.

claims.text: Martin Luther King Jr. and other leaders of the civil rights movement were educated in a system that emphasized shared, specific factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: In high school he was raised on Baker and Thorndike or the equivalent. He knew which buttons to push, because our “mere fact” education had supplied most Americans with those same buttons.

claims.text: Massive positive social changes, such as the civil rights movement, require participants who are intellectually and socially competent.
claims.source_passage: How else can such a massive change for the better as the civil rights movement be accomplished except by people who are intellectually and socially competent?

claims.text: The demonization of political opponents in modern discourse is a direct result of ignorance and the use of slogans over thought.
claims.source_passage: The demonization of the other in our politics is the result of ignorance, and of living by slogan.

claims.text: Decisions regarding shared educational content in a democracy should be made by the majority.
claims.source_passage: The unexceptionable answer would be: “The majority should decide, as is normal in a democracy.”

claims.text: The risk of thought control in a centralized curriculum is mitigated as long as the content-selection process and the results are fully public.
claims.source_passage: Doesn’t that raise the specter of thought control?” No, so long as the process and the results are fully public.

claims.text: Any jurisdiction that adopts a well-thought-out topic-specific K–8 curriculum will inevitably see improvements in achievement and equity.
claims.source_passage: Whatever the concrete topic sequence determined by whatever patriotic and savvy group, any city or state that adopted a well-thought-out topic-specific K–8 curriculum and classroom materials would begin topping the charts in achievement and equity.

claims.text: Public support for a common curriculum will increase once the feared negative consequences fail to appear.
claims.source_passage: Elsewhere, the public would demand to follow suit, especially when none of the supposed evil consequences of commonality appeared.

claims.text: Knowledge of a specific subject is a more critical requirement for good teaching than knowledge of pedagogical techniques.
claims.source_passage: The first requirement for good teaching at every level is knowledge of the specific subject, not knowledge of pedagogical technique.

claims.text: The most valuable form of teacher training, after subject knowledge, is practical advice from experienced peers on teaching specific topics to specific age groups.
claims.source_passage: The second-most-valuable teacher-training element is advice from experienced teachers about particular approaches that have worked well with that topic for each specific age group.

claims.text: A content-specific curriculum will increase teacher satisfaction by providing better training, support materials, and a sense of contributing to a positive national outcome.
claims.source_passage: Not only would they be given more adequate training and support materials, but they also would have an emotional stake in the positive outcome for their state and country.

claims.text: Parental demand for a shared content sequence will become inescapable once the results of such a system are demonstrated.
claims.source_passage: The results and the logic will prove inescapable. People elsewhere in the nation will conclude that much of the content sequence should be concrete and shared in their states as well.

claims.text: Objections to commonality and content specificity are reactionary rather than forward-looking.
claims.source_passage: The tired slogans used against commonality and content specificity will lose their hold. They will be seen as reactionary, not forward looking.

claims.text: It is true that major educational initiatives like the Common Core naturally require a significant amount of time to show measurable effects on student outcomes.
claims.source_passage: Its advocates acknowledge that failure but say: 'It’s the early days. These things take time.' That’s true.

claims.text: It is impossible to completely eliminate student boredom in a classroom with mixed levels of prior knowledge.
claims.source_passage: It’s never possible in a mixed class to overcome boredom entirely. But it’s desirable to reduce boredom by ensuring that all students enter the class knowing addition.

claims.text: Human thinking is encoded symbolically in the neocortex, and these symbols are capable of being translated into words.
claims.source_passage: Much of our thinking is coded symbolically in the neocortex, and those symbols can be translated into words.

claims.text: Education should not adopt inferior principles simply because they are politically or commercially convenient.
claims.source_passage: We should not accept inferior educational principles just because they are politically and commercially convenient. Nor should we pretend that inferior principles are superior ones.

claims.text: American educators adhere to inferior pedagogical principles out of an earnest belief in inherited traditions rather than a lack of goodwill.
claims.source_passage: To be fair, our educators have been following inferior principles partly because the progressive traditions that we have inherited from the first half of the twentieth century, though incorrect, are still earnestly adhered to.

claims.text: Widespread, democratically shared knowledge within a population serves as a technical safeguard against the danger of thought control.
claims.source_passage: In response to the widely heard claim that mandating specific statewide or nationwide topics will lead to thought control, parents and other citizens should respond that broad knowledge leads to less danger of thought control.

claims.text: The perceived difficulty of a 'complex' text is actually a function of the student's familiarity with the subject matter rather than an inherent quality of the text.
claims.source_passage: Textual complexity is not a scientifically valid criterion, because textual complexity may be quite easy to the student when the subject matter is familiar, and textual simplicity quite difficult when the subject matter is unfamiliar.

claims.text: The 1952 SAT verbal scores were higher than modern scores because students then possessed more of the shared knowledge required for social communication.
claims.source_passage: The superior verbal skills demonstrated by American students on the SAT verbal test back in 1952 owed to their having more of the shared knowledge needed for social communication than the students of 2012.

claims.text: The non-specialist knowledge base of educated Americans provides a viable starting point for designing a grade-school content sequence.
claims.source_passage: It made sense that this nonspecialist knowledge base of educated Americans could serve as a start. Then it could be broken down into topics that might belong exclusively to high school and college.

claims.text: The use of shared factual knowledge in education enables individuals to recognize and escape the dominant prejudices of their local social environment.
claims.source_passage: it enabled me to read An American Dilemma by Gunnar Myrdal, which opened my eyes and allowed me to escape the dominant prejudices around me. That is, it enabled just the opposite of thought control!

evidence.description: Standardized test scores and achievement gap data post-implementation of Common Core.
The logical sequence of mathematics instruction (addition before multiplication).
Global educational performance data confirming the effectiveness of coherent, specific curricula.
Singapore's #1 ranking in PISA for reading, math, and science.
Singapore's approved textbook list, specifically the 'Character and Citizenship Education' and 'My Pals Are Here! Science' series published by Marshall Cavendish Education.
The thematic curriculum structure of Singaporean science textbooks which link life and physical sciences to show relationships.
Testimony from freelance writer John Riddle in 'Writers Weekly' regarding the superficial and rapid process of writing American history textbooks.
evidence.source_passage: I replied, “I’ll take the Pony Express for two thousand dollars, please.” . . . I could not believe how easy it was to write for those publishers.

evidence.description: The author's personal experience being offered low-quality work-for-hire book projects by educational publishers.
Singaporean student performance on international assessments (reading, math, science), showing both high average scores and high equity regardless of parental SES.
A comparison of American SAT verbal scores from 1952 to 2012, showing a significant decline.
The 1990 Charlottesville conference where 100 experts ratified a K-6 content sequence.
The adoption of the Core Knowledge Sequence by approximately 2,000 schools nationwide.
The use of the Core Knowledge English Language Arts (ELA) program in 4,000 schools.
Canada's high performance in PISA reading rankings is cited as evidence that language arts should not be treated as a separate skill.
The author's personal experience in Memphis in the 1930s, where a nation-centered curriculum led to high literacy and critical thinking.
Martin Luther King Jr.'s education was based on a common-fact model (Baker and Thorndike), enabling him to effectively communicate and lead social change.
Historical account of American verbal scores previously being in the top group in international rankings.
The leadership of Martin Luther King Jr. as an example of social change facilitated by a common, knowledge-rich education.
High parental demand for entry into Jeffrey Litt’s schools in the South Bronx.

concepts.author_definition: A group (like a classroom) whose members understand one another because they share relevant background knowledge, allowing for effective communication.
The structural property of a curriculum where topics are sequenced over time to ensure students possess the prior knowledge required for new learning.
The state of shared educational content across a group of students to ensure social inclusion and effective group communication.
The degree to which curriculum standards define actual content and topics, which Hirsch argues is necessary to achieve commonality.
A social art inherently connected to society that holds a group together through shared language and shared knowledge, functioning as a tool for acculturation.
concepts.common_alternative: A set of abstract, transferable skills often categorized as 'Language Arts.'

concepts.author_definition: A term from Durkheim referring to the character and citizenship education that defines a society's values and norms.
A specific, explicit roadmap of the factual content to be taught in each grade, essential for achieving coherence and fairness.
concepts.common_alternative: Broad, content-neutral skills-based standards like 'analyzing text.'

concepts.author_definition: An invalid criterion in the Common Core that treats reading difficulty as inherent to a text rather than dependent on a student's prior knowledge of the subject matter.
The false idea that skills like 'reading a novel' or 'reading complex texts' are abstract abilities that can be taught separately from specific factual knowledge.
A curriculum that is intentionally designed to build specific knowledge across grade levels in a coherent, integrated fashion rather than focusing on abstract skills.
A grade-by-grade curriculum guide that specifies the topics and vocabulary children should learn in elementary school to ensure literacy and equity.
A skill, like language proficiency, that cannot be performed in isolation from a specific body of background knowledge.
Curricular content chosen based on individual child interests or local preferences rather than a coherent, shared sequence.
A curriculum focused on shared national history, foundational documents, and common cultural facts intended to unify the student body.
A patriotic, thoughtful, and stable middle ground in politics that is maintained by a citizenry with shared knowledge and reduced susceptibility to demagoguery.
The fear, often raised by critics, that a unified curriculum allows a small elite to manipulate the thinking of the entire population.
A derogatory term used by critics for content-rich education, which Hirsch defends as the necessary 'buttons' for effective communication and social action.
A symbolic mental code in the neocortex that can be translated into words, representing how subjects like math or engineering are stored in the mind.
A late-twentieth-century tradition and ideological framework that prioritizes individual child-centered learning over common curriculum and content specificity.
A set of educational theories currently used by school administrators that emphasize individual knowledge construction over shared, sequential content.
A commercial practice in educational publishing where freelance writers are hired quickly to produce content with minimal subject-matter vetting.
The core topics and vocabulary shared by literate citizens, serving as the foundational content for a general education curriculum.

cases.name: Singapore
cases.description: A multiracial, multicultural nation of 5.6 million people that has achieved the top rank in PISA reading, math, and science. Its success is attributed to an elementary curriculum that balances content, skills, and values through a specific list of approved textbooks.
key_passages: Singapore—an obvious move, since that country has been number one in the PISA rankings in all three of the PISA subjects: reading, math, and science.
Never mind that Singapore is a multiracial, multicultural country that must be doing something right in its primary schools.

Singapore’s introductory document for the elementary grades turns out to be a marvelous description of an ideal elementary education, one that strikes a balance of content mastery, skills, and values.

cases.name: American Textbook Publishing
cases.description: An industry characterized by the hiring of non-expert freelance writers through online ads to produce educational content rapidly. It prioritizes the production of numerous new titles for profit over the quality and vetting of the content.
key_passages: American educational publishers advertise for writers on websites like Craigslist and freelance job boards.

What we’re left with are quickly produced books filled with errors and omissions, and rather than spending the money to perfect a small number of books, publishers have found it more profitable to keep churning out new ones each year.

cases.name: US SAT Scores 1952 vs 2012
cases.description: A comparison used to show that previous generations of American students possessed greater shared knowledge for social communication, which resulted in higher verbal scores than modern students, despite the lack of 'skills-based' training in the past.
key_passages: The superior verbal skills demonstrated by American students on the SAT verbal test back in 1952 owed to their having more of the shared knowledge needed for social communication than the students of 2012.

cases.name: Miami-Dade County Reform (1990)
cases.description: Under Superintendent Paul Bell, the fourth-largest school district in the US planned to implement a content-rich sequence across all its diverse schools. Bell argued that regardless of initial test results, the substance of the curriculum would bridge the achievement gap. However, Bell died of a heart attack shortly after committing to the plan, and the author views this as a historical 'lost chance' where a major district could have proven the model's efficacy to the nation.
key_passages: He introduced himself to the group as Paul Bell, superintendent of schools for Miami-Dade County, a huge and highly diverse school district
He said that this sequence was going to be put into full effect in the schools of Miami-Dade within two years
I still think of that unique pioneering effort by someone in high authority as a lost chance for our public schooling

cases.name: Canada
cases.description: Canada is highlighted for its high PISA rankings in reading, which Hirsch attributes to a curriculum that does not treat language arts as a content-free skill.
key_passages: Canada, which is tied for second place in the PISA rankings in reading, makes it clear that language arts is not a separate skill.

cases.name: Memphis, Tennessee (1930s)
cases.description: The author describes his own education in the 1930s as nation-centered and rich in common facts, which he argues produced capable readers and independent thinkers despite the social context of racism.
key_passages: The curriculum that I received was nation centered, not child centered. We learned US history. We honored the American founders... all that 'useless' shared knowledge did turn us into quite good readers.

cases.name: The Civil Rights Movement
cases.description: The author argues the movement succeeded because its leaders and the broader public shared a common intellectual background that allowed leaders to communicate effectively and push 'buttons' that resonated across society.
key_passages: How else can such a massive change for the better as the civil rights movement be accomplished except by people who are intellectually and socially competent?

cases.name: Jeffrey Litt’s schools in the South Bronx
cases.description: A contemporary example of schools utilizing a structured, content-rich curriculum that generates massive parental demand, serving as a model for the success of Hirsch's proposed reforms.
key_passages: That parental demand is what happens by the thousands every year with Jeffrey Litt’s schools in the South Bronx.

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: Philosophical principles of education, often associated with child-centeredness in the US but praised in Singapore's documents.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch notes with some surprise and approval that Singapore's highly successful curriculum documents include praise for Dewey, suggesting Deweyan ideas can coexist with content mastery.
key_passages: It is well written, and it even offers a special word of praise for John Dewey!

thinkers.name: Émile Durkheim
thinkers.key_idea: The concept of 'moralité' as the foundation of character and citizenship education.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Durkheim's terminology to justify the Singaporean model of 'Character and Citizenship Education' as more sociologically accurate than the American 'Language Arts.'
key_passages: it is intimately connected to what Durkheim called moralité, which can very accurately be translated as “character and citizenship education.”

thinkers.name: Paul Bell
thinkers.key_idea: The implementation of a content-rich, coherent curriculum is more important than short-term test scores and is what parents and teachers actually desire.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch treats Bell as a tragic hero of the movement—a leader who had the political will and authority to enact the Core Knowledge vision at scale.
key_passages: He said he did not care what the initial tests results would say, because he knew... that this approach would ultimately make the key difference for all students.
Paul would have prevailed. He was a powerfully magnetic person who knew the ropes.

thinkers.name: Joseph Kett
thinkers.key_idea: Co-authored the master list of topics and words known to literate Americans which formed the basis for the Cultural Literacy list.
thinkers.engagement: Collaborator in the early research phase of identifying communal knowledge.
key_passages: Some initial preparatory work had been done over the prior three years, when professors Joseph Kett and James Trefil and I worked out a master list of topics and words

thinkers.name: James Trefil
thinkers.key_idea: Co-authored the master list of topics and words known to literate Americans.
thinkers.engagement: Collaborator providing subject-matter expertise for the initial knowledge lists.
key_passages: professors Joseph Kett and James Trefil and I worked out a master list of topics and words

thinkers.name: Horace Mann
thinkers.key_idea: Mann argued that the purpose of early public schools was forming a common citizenship, which aligns with viewing reading as more than a mechanical skill.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch links his proposal for a knowledge-based curriculum to Mann's original vision for American public education.
key_passages: To think of literature in that way is not far from what Horace Mann was getting at in his reports about forming our early public schools in Massachusetts.

thinkers.name: Gunnar Myrdal
thinkers.key_idea: Author of An American Dilemma, a study of race relations in the US.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Myrdal's book as an example of a complex text that he could only understand because of his factual, common-knowledge education.
key_passages: it enabled me to read An American Dilemma by Gunnar Myrdal, which opened my eyes and allowed me to escape the dominant prejudices around me.

thinkers.name: Martin Luther King Jr.
thinkers.key_idea: The successful civil rights leader was a product of a content-rich, fact-based education that provided him the tools for social change.
thinkers.engagement: The author uses King as the primary exemplar of the civic benefits of a shared, traditional education.
key_passages: In high school he was raised on Baker and Thorndike or the equivalent. He knew which buttons to push, because our “mere fact” education had supplied most Americans with those same buttons.

actors.name: Bill Gates / Gates Foundation
actors.context: Philanthropic supporters of the Common Core initiative.

actors.name: Marshall Cavendish Education
actors.context: The publisher of the approved textbook series used in Singapore’s schools.

actors.name: Marshall Cavendish Education
actors.context: The publisher responsible for Singapore's mandated national textbook series.

actors.name: Mason Crest Publishing
actors.context: An American publisher used as an example of hiring low-cost freelance writers for history textbooks.

actors.name: John Riddle
actors.context: A freelance writer who described the ease and lack of rigor in writing American educational books.

actors.name: Educational Publishers
actors.context: Accused of prioritizing profitability and churning out low-quality, error-filled books over educational perfection.

actors.name: State Governors
actors.context: Identified as potential leaders who could win public backing for specific grade-by-grade topic sequences.

actors.name: School Administrators
actors.context: Characterized as being trained in 'romantic individualism' and 'constructivist principles' which hinder the adoption of content-specific curricula.

actors.name: Miami-Dade County Public Schools
actors.context: The district intended to be the first major implementer of the Core Knowledge Sequence.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: The organization that emerged from the Charlottesville conference to provide the K-8 sequence.

actors.name: Martin Luther King Jr.
actors.context: Used as an example of a leader whose effective activism was grounded in a common-knowledge education.

actors.name: Paul Vallas
actors.context: Referred to as 'Paul,' who attempted to implement a content-rich topic list in Miami-Dade County.

actors.name: Martin Luther King Jr.
actors.context: Cited as an example of the effectiveness of shared knowledge in achieving social justice.

actors.name: Jeffrey Litt
actors.context: Administrator of successful schools in the South Bronx that use a content-rich curriculum.

actors.name: The Majority
actors.context: Identified as the democratic body that should decide curriculum content.

objections_raised.objection: The failure of the Common Core to improve scores is simply due to it being 'early days'; the initiative just needs more time.
objections_raised.response: While time is a factor, the fundamental reason for failure is the lack of specific content in the standards.

objections_raised.objection: Mandating specific statewide or nationwide topics will lead to thought control.
objections_raised.response: Broad knowledge actually reduces the danger of thought control. Knowledgeable students, like those in Singapore, are more empowered and less easily manipulated.

objections_raised.objection: A content-rich curriculum might not show immediate improvements on standardized tests.
objections_raised.response: Bell argued that the long-term impact on student understanding and equity was more important than initial test results and would eventually show up in high school outcomes and the NAEP.

objections_raised.objection: Common content will lead to thought control, political control, and loss of independence.
objections_raised.response: The author argues there is no evidence for this; historically, a common curriculum enabled the civil rights movement and independent thought by providing the tools for intellectual competence.

objections_raised.objection: Who should decide what we should all know?
objections_raised.response: The majority should decide, as is normal in a democracy.

objections_raised.objection: Trusting a small elite group with curriculum power raises the specter of thought control.
objections_raised.response: This is avoided as long as the content selection process and the results are fully public.

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: We managed at the Boar’s Head Inn in 1990.
